stratum: Algebra I: fields & univariate polynomials *(the engine's substrate)*
assumed_background: Ch 7 assumes finite-field arithmetic, polynomial degree, roots, and evaluation.
Lagrange interpolation has explained how values and low-degree polynomials determine one another under a degree promise.
learning_objectives: Describe Reed-Solomon encoding as evaluating a low-degree message polynomial on many field points.
Compute a concrete F_17 codeword from message coefficients.
Explain distance: two degree-<k polynomials agree on at most k-1 evaluation points.
Connect Reed-Solomon fingerprinting to low-degree testing, FRI, and polynomial commitments.

graphify_subgraph.interpretation: The query landed in the encoding/performance and FRI neighborhoods, tying Reed-Solomon proximity to STARK/FRI and the Layer 4 arithmetization path.
